steps.title: What Telos is
steps.narration: Telos is the primary engine that takes the stage. It is a local-first workbench that speaks MCP and brings every other flagship together under one roof.
steps.command: echo telos - local-first MCP workbench that unifies every flagship into one engine
steps.stdout: telos - local-first MCP workbench that unifies every flagship into one engine
steps.output: telos - local-first MCP workbench that unifies every flagship into one engine

steps.title: The full capability map
steps.narration: Here is the wonder of it: sixty-nine tools across gather, index, forum, crucible, and telos itself, all reachable through one stdio server on your own machine. This is the whole spine, gathered into a single surface.
steps.stdout:
Project Telos MCP Catalog
tools    69 total, 69 available
transport stdio, streamable-http
gather    5 tools gather.status, gather.doctor, +3 more
index     5 tools index.map, index.context, +3 more
forum     5 tools forum.route, forum.ledger.summary, +3 more
crucible  13 tools crucible.status, crucible.doctor, +11 more
telos     41 tools telos.status, telos.doctor, +39 more
next     node demo/catalog.mjs
steps.output:
Project Telos MCP Catalog

telos     41 tools telos.status, telos.doctor, +39 more
next     node demo/catalog.mjs

steps.title: A readiness check
steps.narration: Telos runs its own doctor and reports each check as a verdict. The engine that hosts everyone else keeps a live readiness score on itself, right down to the certificate demo and the honest unverifiable path.

steps.title: A status receipt
steps.narration: And it emits its state as a structured receipt with a schema and a verdict. Not a screenshot of health, but a record you can re-read and re-check whenever you want.
